creator: dch@freebsd.org
text:
I've finally gotten updated images available in the marketplace. I
couldn't find how to make these privately available, so .. they are
public.

These are still experimental, built by my own dusty hands, but I
think we'll have everything ready to bang out official RE-generated
images as part of 14.2-RELEASE cycle.

I have built & published only 14.1-RELEASE-p5 ZFS images on arm64 &
amd64 we will have UFS and ZFS flavours ready for you about a week
after 14.2-RELEASE, as Oracle's validation process takes about 5
days, and another day to publish/release the images across regions.

Currently I've found 2 minor issues:

- the images are built to 14.1-RELEASE-p5 but still download the
  same patches on boot, this is a reproducible build issue on my side,
  and won't be present for the upcoming 14.2-RELEASE builds
- the cloud-init config still uses /usr/home/$USER while 14.0-RELEASE
  and up actually uses /home/$USER now. This is also cosmetic but offends
  my technical OCD

Please test these, but remember that once 14.2-RELEASE is out I will
retire these in favour of the official Release Engineering built images.

UFS images will come after I've resolved these 2 above minor issues,
but will still take a week to appear because of Oracle's validation
process.

Report any issues here, thanks!

If anybody explicitly needs 13.x images, let me know, otherwise I
probably won't do them.
